en.extract:
Lühder's bushshrike (Laniarius luehderi) is a species of bird (a shrike) in the family Malaconotidae. It is found in Angola, Burundi, Cameroon, Republic of the Congo, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon, Kenya, Nigeria, Rwanda, South Sudan, Tanzania, and Uganda. The common name and Latin binomial commemorate the German naturalist ornithologist W. Lühder.

'''Lühder's bushshrike''' ('''''Laniarius luehderi''''') is a species of [[bird]] (a [[shrike]]) in the family [[Malaconotidae]]. It is found in [[Angola]], [[Burundi]], [[Cameroon]], [[Republic of the Congo]], [[Democratic Republic of the Congo]], [[Equatorial Guinea]], [[Gabon]], [[Kenya]], [[Nigeria]], [[Rwanda]], [[South Sudan]], [[Tanzania]], and [[Uganda]]. The common name and Latin binomial commemorate the German naturalist ornithologist [[W. Lühder]].<ref>{{cite book|last=Beolens|first=Bo|title=Whose Bird? Men and Women Commemorated in the Common Names of Birds|year=2003|publisher=Christopher Helm|location=London|page=214|author2=Watkins, Michael }}</ref>
